original_image_tags: A dark, high-contrast scan of a typewritten document on dark paper (or a dark photocopy/negative). The main visible text is a single paragraph recounting a sighting by John Snyder. The rest of the page is heavily obscured, appearing as faint, ghosted, or unreadable lines of text in the lower right quadrant, suggesting bleed-through, poor copy quality, or partial redaction. A handwritten number "161" is visible in the bottom right corner. Two hole-punch marks are visible on the left margin.

elements.description: A page featuring a typed account of an observation by John Snyder, a former pilot, regarding an object on August 6, 1947. The text is bordered by 'RESTRICTED' stamps at the top and bottom, with faint, illegible text from the reverse side visible through the paper.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content as a typed document with a single paragraph about a John Snyder sighting, but Description B adds the detail of 'RESTRICTED' stamps which Description A does not mention, and this is not a contradiction but an omission/addition.
